# Hardware Intelligence Report — 2026-07-13

**Generated by:** elect-rix hardware-intel cron feed  
**Focus:** Efficiency-optimized compute for local inference (TOPS/W, not raw perf)  
**Filters:** Consumer <$500 / SMB <$2000, <50W consumer preference, can run 7B+ at ≥10 tok/s  
**Excluded:** Data center GPUs, cloud-only, vaporware without ship dates  
**Baseline:** [hw-intel-2026-07-06.md](./hw-intel-2026-07-06.md)

---

## 1. New Hardware Found / Major Status Changes

### NVIDIA DGX Spark (shipping — previously “needs deep-dive”)
- **Specs:** NVIDIA GB10 Grace Blackwell superchip, **128GB unified LPDDR5x**, 4TB NVMe, ~150×150×50.5 mm desktop form factor
- **Claimed capability:** Build/run autonomous agents; work with models up to ~200B params (quantized); fine-tune up to ~70B
- **Price:** **~$4,699** (NVIDIA Marketplace); third-party listings ~$4,000–$4,799; hard to find / sold out at launch (Oct 2025), still constrained stock mid-2026
- **Power:** ~140W class (desktop “personal supercomputer,” not <50W)
- **Performance (public consolidation, Q4 single-stream):**  
  - 7B: ~105–125 tok/s  
  - 13B: ~75–95 tok/s  
  - 30B: ~50–65 tok/s  
  - 70B: ~35–45 tok/s  
  - ~120B-class: ~20–28 tok/s  
- **elect-rix note:** **Above SMB $2k budget.** Full CUDA/NVIDIA stack + 128GB unified memory is excellent for large-model residency without multi-GPU hacks. Treat as **Tier 3 / prosumer reference** (same tier as AGX Thor, high-end Mac Studio). Bandwidth (~273 GB/s LPDDR5x) is lower than discrete RTX 5090 GDDR7, so peak 7B speed trails a 5090; the win is **capacity** for 30B–70B+ full-residency.  
- **Source:** [NVIDIA DGX Spark](https://www.nvidia.com/en-us/products/workstations/dgx-spark/), [Marketplace ~$4,699](https://marketplace.nvidia.com/en-us/enterprise/personal-ai-supercomputers/dgx-spark/), [Presenc AI 2026 tps consolidation](https://presenc.ai/research/local-llm-tokens-per-second-benchmarks-2026)

### Apple M5 Ultra / Mac Studio refresh (not shipping — delayed)
- **Status:** Still **rumored / delayed**, not announced for sale as of mid-July 2026.
- **Reports:** Mac Studio refresh postponed (memory supply + pricing); Bloomberg/MacRumors timeline shifted toward **~October 2026** (was earlier 2026). Some WWDC-2026 rumor pieces claimed M5 Ultra unveil; product still not in channel.
- **Rumored specs (treat as soft):** Dual M5 Max-class fuse → ~36 CPU cores / high GPU core counts; **up to ~768GB unified RAM** in top configs (aggressive rumor); efficiency still Apple-class.
- **elect-rix note:** Remains **Watch List #1**. Do not plan deployments around M5 Ultra until ship + street price. Current best Apple local-LLM boxes remain **M5 Max Mac Studio / MacBook Pro** (shipping) and older **M3/M4 Ultra** stock if available.  
- **Source:** [MacRumors Jun 25 2026](https://www.macrumors.com/2026/06/25/m5-ultra-mac-studio-2026/), [Notebookcheck WWDC rumor](https://www.notebookcheck.net/Mac-Studio-with-Apple-M5-Ultra-to-be-unveiled-at-WWDC-report-reveals-specs.1317931.0.html)

### AMD Radeon RX 9070 / 9070 XT (RDNA4) — consumer discrete for local LLM
- **Specs:** RDNA4; **RX 9070 XT ~16GB** (no 24GB+ RDNA4 consumer card yet); ROCm 7.2 lists official consumer support for 9070 family
- **Performance (community):** Llama 3.1 8B class ~**90 tok/s** on 9070 XT (near 7900 XTX); Vulkan llama-server sometimes beats half-broken ROCm paths (e.g. ~62 vs ~48 tok/s in one RDNA4 write-up). **7900 XTX (24GB) still better for 13B–30B** because of VRAM headroom.
- **Price / power:** Competitive mid-range; ~300W class — **exceeds 50W consumer edge target** (desktop GPU tier)
- **elect-rix note:** Valid **budget desktop accelerator** for 7B–14B if shop already has AMD host. **Not preferred for greenfield elect-rix** vs Jetson (power) or unified-memory mini-PCs (model size). Prefer **24GB cards (7900 XTX used/new)** when discrete AMD is required.  
- **Source:** [LocalAI Master ROCm 2026](https://localaimaster.com/blog/amd-rocm-local-llm-setup), [IDFS AMD inference 2026](https://idfs.ai/blog/amd-gpus-for-ai-inference-2026), [digtvbg RDNA4 Vulkan vs ROCm](https://digtvbg.com/blog/llama-server-vulkan-rdna4-vllm-rocm-benchmark/)

### NVIDIA RTX 5070 Ti (Blackwell consumer) — value discrete
- **Specs:** 16GB GDDR7, ~1,406 AI TOPS, ~300W TDP, street **~$750–$950**
- **Performance:** **7B–14B at ~90–120+ tok/s** (often cited ~105 tok/s Llama 3 8B Q4); ~90% of practical 5090 inference for small models at <½ price. **70B does not fit** in 16GB without heavy offload.
- **elect-rix note:** Best **price/perf discrete** for 7B–13B interactive speeds if wall power and desktop form factor are OK. Fails consumer <50W filter; fine as optional **SMB desktop node** alongside unified-memory boxes. Dual-5070 Ti builds appear in hobbyist agent rigs.  
- **Source:** [Compute Market 5070 Ti local AI](https://www.compute-market.com/blog/rtx-5070-ti-local-ai-2026), [LocalAI Master hardware guide](https://localaimaster.com/blog/ai-hardware-requirements-2025-complete-guide)

### Hailo-10H / Raspberry Pi AI HAT+ 2 (refresh of prior entry)
- **Confirmed shipping:** $130 HAT; **~40 TOPS INT4**, **8GB onboard LPDDR4X**, max ~3W for accelerator; total Pi 5 stack ~$210
- **LLM reality:** Strong for **1–3B** / vision+caption / on-device GenAI demos; **not** a reliable 7B ≥10 tok/s platform. Software still rough (package lag, model churn).
- **elect-rix note:** Unchanged tier — **lightweight / special-env only**, not primary 7B deployment.  
- **Source:** [Jeff Geerling AI HAT+ 2](https://www.jeffgeerling.com/blog/2026/raspberry-pi-ai-hat-2/), [Hailo-10H product](https://hailo.ai/products/ai-accelerators/hailo-10h-ai-accelerator/), [Hailo GenAI blog](https://hailo.ai/blog/bringing-on-device-generative-ai-to-the-pi-when-and-why-youll-need-the-raspberry-pi-ai-hat-2/)

## 2. Updates to Previously Tracked Devices

| Device | Status | Price | 7B-class tok/s | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Jetson Orin Nano Super (8GB)** | Shipping | **$249** | **~15–22 tok/s** (3B–8B Q4; ~20 on Llama 3.x 8B INT4/MLC paths) | 67 TOPS, ~7–15W, 102 GB/s. Still **#1 consumer edge** pick. |
| Jetson AGX Thor | Shipping | $3,499 | Very high / 128GB | Above SMB budget; prosumer edge reference. |
| **Framework Desktop / Ryzen AI Max+ 395 64GB** | Shipping | **~$1,659** | **~45–52 tok/s** (8B Q4; community) | **#1 SMB pick.** 128GB SKU ~$3,149. |
| Framework / Strix Halo **128GB** | Shipping | ~$2,000–$3,149 | 70B Q4 ~**26–32 tok/s**; 32B ~22; 14B ~32 | Unified memory wins for 30B–70B without discrete VRAM. |
| Apple M5 MacBook Air | Shipping | $1,099+ | Good (RAM-limited) | Consumer entry efficiency. |
| Apple M5 Pro / M5 Max | Shipping | $1,999+ / higher | 7B high (M5 Max 128GB ~**95–110** MLX); 70B M4/M5 Max ~**18–32** | Best TOPS/W; mature Metal/MLX. |
| Mac Studio M5 Ultra | **Not shipping** | TBD | TBD | Delayed toward late 2026; watch only. |
| **NVIDIA DGX Spark** | Shipping (stock tight) | **~$4,699** | 7B ~105–125; 70B ~35–45 | Tier 3 reference; 128GB unified + CUDA. |
| Raspberry Pi 5 + AI HAT+ 2 | Shipping | ~$210 | <10 on 7B | 1–3B only for usable UX. |
| Orange Pi 5 Pro (RK3588) | Shipping | ~$120 | **1.5–5 tok/s** on 3B–7B (CPU/NPU paths) | Below ≥10 tok/s 7B bar; OK for tiny models. |
| Snapdragon X Elite | Shipping (OEM laptops) | OEM | ~15–30 class (bandwidth-bound) | NPU still weak for large LLM; CPU/iGPU carry decode. |
| Snapdragon X2 Elite | Emerging / shipping OEMs | OEM | ~**+13%** decode headroom vs X Elite (~34 vs ~30 ceiling on 8B math) | 80 TOPS NPU; bandwidth only ~152 GB/s — **TOPS ≠ tok/s**. |
| Intel Arc B580 | Shipping | **~$249–$289** | Usable 7B (Vulkan/IPEX); dual-card hobby builds | Software still second-tier vs CUDA; budget desktop only. |
| RTX 5070 Ti 16GB | Shipping | ~$750–$950 | **~90–120+** | Desktop power; best mid discrete for 7–14B. |
| RTX 5090 32GB | Shipping | ~$2k–$5k street | **~130–150** (7B); 70B offload-limited | Peak speed; power/price high. |
| RX 7900 XTX 24GB | Shipping / used | Variable | **~82–96** (8B) | Best AMD consumer VRAM for 13B–30B. |
| RX 9070 XT 16GB | Shipping | Mid-range | **~90** (8B) | RDNA4; VRAM caps model size. |
| Tenstorrent Wormhole | Dev kits / workstations | Cards + **$12k+** boxes | Niche | Above budget; open-ish stack; watch for cheaper cards. |

### Strix Halo / Framework — measured community numbers (consolidated)
From LocalAI Master + Framework community (ROCm/Vulkan, 128GB UMA, typical Q4):

| Workload | ~tok/s |
|---|---|
| Llama 3.2 1B Q4 | ~110 |
| Llama 3.2 3B Q4 | ~78 |
| Qwen 2.5 7B / Llama 3.1 8B Q4 | **~48–52** |
| Qwen 2.5 14B Q4 | ~32 |
| Qwen 2.5 32B INT4 | ~22 |
| Llama 3.3 70B Q4 / Q5 | **~26–32 / ~26** |
| Mixtral 8x7B Q4 | ~38 |
| Large MoE / partial-fit 100B+ | single-digit–low teens (config-dependent) |

**Cluster note:** AMD published multi-node Strix Halo RPC + ROCm paths for **~1T-class** models at ~8–9.5 tok/s gen — research/demo, not single-box SMB.  
**Source:** [Strix Halo guide](https://localaimaster.com/blog/strix-halo-ai-max-395-guide), [AMD trillion-param article](https://www.amd.com/en/developer/resources/technical-articles/2026/how-to-run-a-one-trillion-parameter-llm-locally-an-amd.html), Framework Desktop community threads

### Apple single-stream (Presenc AI consolidation, May–Jul 2026 data)
| Hardware | 7B Q4 | 13B | 30B | 70B | ~120B |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| RTX 5090 32GB | 130–150 | 85–105 | 40–55* | 14–22* | OOM* |
| DGX Spark 128GB | 105–125 | 75–95 | 50–65 | **35–45** | 20–28 |
| Mac Studio M5 Max 128GB | 95–110 | 65–85 | 40–52 | 25–32 | 14–19 |
| Mac Studio M5 Ultra 192GB (est./rumor table) | 120–140 | 85–105 | 55–70 | 32–42 | 20–26 |
| Mac Studio M4 Max 128GB | 75–90 | 50–65 | 30–40 | 18–24 | 10–14 |

\*70B Q4 exceeds 32GB VRAM → offload penalties on 5090.  
**Source:** [Presenc AI tps 2026](https://presenc.ai/research/local-llm-tokens-per-second-benchmarks-2026)

### Jetson Orin Nano Super
- Price floor **$249**; software “Super” mode → **67 TOPS**, **102 GB/s**
- Real-world Ollama/MLC paths: **~20–22 tok/s** on small Llama 3.x (3B–8B class), meeting elect-rix **≥10 tok/s on 7B** with headroom on lighter quants  
**Source:** [NVIDIA Orin Nano Super kit](https://www.nvidia.com/en-us/autonomous-machines/embedded-systems/jetson-orin/nano-super-developer-kit/), [NVIDIA Super boost blog](https://developer.nvidia.com/blog/nvidia-jetson-orin-nano-developer-kit-gets-a-super-boost/), community Ollama reports

---

## 3. Market Signals

- **Edge AI market (multiple firms, 2026 bases):** roughly **$25–47B in 2026** depending on scope, with **~15–32% CAGR** into early 2030s (hardware-heavy share still >50% in several reports). Consumer electronics + automotive remain largest edge-chip volume drivers; **local inference privacy/latency** is a stated enterprise driver.  
  - Fortune Business Insights: edge AI **$46.96B (2026) → $445.75B (2034)**, 32.5% CAGR (broad definition).  
  - Grand View / others: more conservative **~$30B (2026) → ~$119B (2033)** class numbers.  
  - Edge **chips** narrower slices: e.g. ~$4.4B (2026) → ~$11.5B (2031).  
- **Personal AI desktops are real products now:** DGX Spark at ~$4–5k and Strix Halo mini-PCs at ~$1.6–3k form a new category between laptops and DGX/cloud.  
- **Memory supply & ASP pressure:** Cited as reason for **Mac Studio M5 delay** and broader Mac price increases — expect unified-memory SKUs to stay premium.  
- **Software stack ranking (unchanged truth, more evidence):**  
  1. **CUDA** (Jetson, RTX, DGX Spark) — still default for production local agents  
  2. **Metal/MLX** (Apple) — best efficiency + polish for single-stream  
  3. **ROCm 7.2** — usable on RDNA3/4 and Strix Halo; still more footguns than CUDA  
  4. **Vulkan** — practical escape hatch on AMD/Intel when ROCm/IPEX lag  
  5. **NPU-first stacks** (Hailo, Hexagon, Rockchip RKNN) — vision/small GenAI; **not** drop-in 7B–13B chat parity  
- **Bandwidth > TOPS for decode:** Snapdragon X2 nearly doubles NPU TOPS vs X Elite but only ~13% more mem BW → similar ~30s tok/s ceilings on 8B. Strix Halo’s wide LPDDR bus is why 70B is practical.  
- **Tenstorrent Wormhole:** Still developer/workstation pricing (**$12k+** systems); not elect-rix deployment hardware.  
- **No consumer Groq LPU device** — still data-center/cloud after NVIDIA acquisition path; filtered out.

---

## 4. Deployment Implications for elect-rix

### Tier 1 — Consumer Edge (<$500, prefer <50W, 7B ≥10 tok/s)
| Device | Verdict |
|---|---|
| **Jetson Orin Nano Super ($249)** | ✅ **Primary.** Mature CUDA, low power, confirmed ~15–22 tok/s on 7B-class Q4 paths. Default elect-rix special-environment node. |
| Raspberry Pi 5 + AI HAT+ 2 (~$210) | ⚠️ **Sub-tier.** Excellent for 1–3B / multimodal triggers; **do not** promise 7B chat UX. |
| Orange Pi 5 / RK3588 (~$120) | ❌ 7B typically **&lt;5–10 tok/s**; NPU stack specialist. Lab only. |
| Arc B580 ($249) in a host | ⚠️ Cheap VRAM but **desktop power + immature stack** — not a portable edge SKU. |

### Tier 2 — SMB / Prosumer (<$2000 preferred, 13B–70B practical)
| Device | Verdict |
|---|---|
| **Framework Desktop Ryzen AI Max+ 395 64GB (~$1,659)** | ✅ **Primary SMB.** Unified memory, shipping, ~50 tok/s on 8B, 30B-class viable. Prefer 64GB for budget; 128GB if 70B is a hard requirement (often pushes >$2k). |
| Other Strix Halo mini-PCs 64GB (~$1.2–1.8k) | ✅ Validate OEM thermals/BIOS UMA settings; same silicon story. |
| **Apple M5 Pro / M5 Max (64–128GB)** | ✅ Best efficiency + MLX reliability. Price often at/above $2k for useful RAM — OK for “premium SMB.” |
| RTX 5070 Ti system (~$1.5–2.5k built) | ✅ If CUDA-only agents and 7–14B peak speed matter more than 70B residency. |
| Used 7900 XTX 24GB host | ✅ AMD path for 13–30B when ROCm/Vulkan is acceptable. |

### Tier 3 — Reference / enterprise edge (>$2k)
- **DGX Spark (~$4.7k):** Personal Blackwell + 128GB + full NVIDIA software — strongest “single box agent lab” if budget allows.  
- **Jetson AGX Thor (~$3.5k):** Robotics/edge Blackwell, 128GB.  
- **Mac Studio M3/M4 Ultra (existing stock) / future M5 Ultra:** Max unified memory for huge local models; M5 Ultra still **not buyable**.  
- **RTX 5090 / multi-GPU:** Peak tok/s, poor efficiency and model-size story vs unified 128GB boxes.

### Key deployment notes (this week)
1. **Promote DGX Spark from “watch” → Tier 3 shipping reference** with hard price and tps bands.  
2. **Do not greenlight M5 Ultra** in quotes or BOM until Apple ships.  
3. **Unified memory remains the elect-rix differentiator** for 30B–70B single-node: Strix Halo 64/128GB and Apple Max/Ultra beat 16GB discrete cards on model size; discrete RTX wins pure 7B tok/s.  
4. **Keep NPU marketing out of 7B RFPs** unless the workload is vision/caption or vendor-certified small LLM.  
5. **Efficiency ranking for always-on agents:** Jetson Orin Nano Super (Tier 1) → Apple Silicon laptops/Studios → Strix Halo mini-PC → discrete RTX/AMD desktops → DGX Spark.

## 5. Watch List (updated)

| Device | Why Watching | Trigger to Promote |
|---|---|---|
| **Apple M5 Ultra / Mac Studio M5** | Delayed; Oct 2026 rumor window; up to very high RAM configs | Official announce + street price + independent 70B tok/s |
| **Jetson Thor T4000 / price cuts** | Could pull Blackwell edge under $2k | Kit price ≤$2,000 and partner availability |
| **Snapdragon X2 Elite LLM (NPU path)** | 80 TOPS; decode still BW-limited | Independent 7B/13B **NPU** tok/s ≥10 with stable Ollama/llama.cpp |
| **ROCm on RDNA4 + Strix Halo** | Usable but uneven vs CUDA | Sustained parity on multi-user vLLM + flash-attn defaults |
| **Intel Arc Pro B-series / multi-B580** | Cheap VRAM density; vLLM posts exist | Production-grade multi-GPU docs + support commitment |
| **Tenstorrent sub-$2k card** | Open-leaning RISC-V AI | Consumer price + llama.cpp-class UX |
| **Hailo larger-memory GenAI SKUs** | 8GB HAT is small-model only | ≥16–32GB local mem + 7B ≥10 tok/s demos |
| **Consumer LPU / Groq-class edge** | Still cloud/datacenter | Any shippable sub-$2k product (unlikely near-term) |

## 6. Delta vs 2026-07-06 Baseline

| Item | Change |
|---|---|
| DGX Spark | **Resolved:** shipping, ~$4.7k, 128GB, tps bands documented → Tier 3 |
| M5 Ultra | Still not shipping; delay to ~Oct 2026 reinforced |
| Strix Halo / Framework | **tok/s filled in** (~48–52 on 8B; ~26–32 on 70B Q4 @128GB) |
| Jetson Orin Nano Super | tok/s confidence raised to **~15–22** on 7B-class paths |
| RTX 5070 Ti / RX 9070 | Added as explicit discrete options with roles |
| Market sizing | Multi-source edge AI growth confirmed; personal AI PCs now a category |
| Recommendations | **Unchanged primary picks:** Jetson Orin Nano Super (Tier 1), Framework 64GB Strix Halo (Tier 2), Apple M5 Pro/Max as efficiency premium |

## 7. Next Run Priorities

1. Street price + lead time tracker for **DGX Spark** and **Framework 64GB vs 128GB**.  
2. Any **Apple Mac Studio M5** invite/FCC/leak that hardens Oct window.  
3. Independent **X2 Elite** 7B/13B benches (CPU vs GPU vs NPU).  
4. **ROCm 7.x** regression/parity notes on 9070 XT and Strix Halo for multi-session agents.  
5. Thor **T4000** availability — first path to Blackwell edge inside SMB envelope.

*Report date: 2026-07-13. Sources are public web/vendor/community benchmarks; tok/s always depend on quant, context length, batch, and runtime (llama.cpp, MLX, vLLM, Ollama, MLC).*
